content.stem: Read this story: 'Dr. Chen watched Arctic terns for three summers. She found out that these birds fly farther than any other animal. They fly from the cold Arctic to the cold Antarctic and back each year. Her work helped other scientists learn how weather changes affect where birds fly.' What was the main reason Dr. Chen did her work?

options.text: To understand how climate affects bird migration
To prove that Arctic terns fly the farthest
To study birds during summer months
To compare Arctic terns with other animals

content.explanation: The passage explicitly states that Dr. Chen's research 'helped scientists understand how climate changes affect bird migration,' which identifies this as the main purpose of her work.

metadata.cognitive_skill: identifying main ideas and distinguishing purpose from supporting details
metadata.misconception_targeted: confusing supporting details with main ideas

distractor_rationale.reasoning: Students might think proving birds fly farthest was the purpose rather than a discovery made along the way
Students might focus on the detail about studying for three summers rather than the research purpose
Students might think comparing animals was the purpose rather than a method used in the research
